exp3_1.conclusion: Under normal market conditions, the strategy achieves near-breakeven in ideal conditions (mean P&L = 0.007%) and marginally negative after friction (mean P&L = -0.025%), with an average slippage cost of 3.1 bps per trade. The limit order fill rate is 47.2%, indicating that ~53% of limit orders require multiple steps or Iron Rule intervention to fill. Iron Rule 3 triggers on 16.0% of trajectories, showing the stop-loss effectively caps downside for the worst-fitting cubic predictions. Iron Rule 5 (emergency market order) was not triggered, confirming that under normal 120-step holding periods the smoothed residual never diverges more than 3.0 sigma from the raw residual.

exp3_2.conclusion: dT_trigger/dδ < 0 strictly holds (slope=-97.4): larger breaks trigger Iron Rule 3 faster. Large breaks (5%+) trigger within 80 seconds on average; 10% breaks trigger instantaneously (T_trigger = 0 min). False positive rate (favorable breaks triggering): 4.9% — the system occasionally triggers on favorable moves, which is acceptable for a risk-averse strategy. Overall trigger rate: 6.0% across all break sizes. Total triggers: 30 out of 500 trajectories.
